trade.summary: Growing bilateral trade. Italia exports machinery, luxury goods, chemicals, and food; Corea del Sud exports electronics, vehicles, and ships. Italian fashion and food are popular in Corea del Sud. Corea del Sudn manufacturing firms source Italian specialty machinery. Corea del Sud's shipbuilding industry competes with Italian luxury yacht sector.

diplomatic.summary: Italia and Corea del Sud enjoy warm diplomatic relations. Both are liberal democracies, OECD members, and important middle powers. Diplomatic contacts have grown with increasing economic integration. Cultural ties through Italian gastronomy, fashion, and design have strong following in Corea del Sud.

military.summary: Limited but growing defense cooperation. Italia is a NATO member; Corea del Sud is a Stati Uniti treaty ally. Some defense technology interest. Italia has participated in multilateral exercises in the Indo-Pacific. No bilateral defense agreement.

financial.summary: Significant bilateral investment. Corea del Sudn conglomerates (Samsung, Hyundai, LG) have Italian operations; Italian luxury firms have significant Corea del Sudn retail presence. Growing investment in manufacturing partnerships.

energy.summary: Both are energy importers. Some cooperation on energy technology and LNG supply chains. No direct bilateral energy trade.

scientific.summary: Active scientific cooperation in engineering, automotive technology, and materials science. Joint academic programs exist. Italian universities have exchange programs with Corea del Sudn counterparts.
